title: bumelia tenax
intro: Bumelia tenax, also known as the Red Bumelia or Bumelia, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Bumelia tenax include relief from fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Bumelia tenax typically involve the roots, stems, and leaves being dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
